title: centaurea triumfettii subsp. variegata
intro: Centaurea triumfettii subsp. variegata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Cornflower or Knawel. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Centaurea triumfettii subsp. variegata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
